name: San Diego (United States)

colors.symbolism: Spain — recalling the Spanish flag and the city's colonial heritage
the central stripe bearing the city seal
the precious metal that helped transform California from a wilderness into a prosperous state, and recalling the Spanish flag

description: An equal vertical tricolour — red (hoist), white (centre), gold (fly) — with a stylised version of the San Diego city seal placed in the centre, entirely within the white stripe.
history: Designed by Albert V. Mayrhofer, who submitted the prototype banner on behalf of the California Historical Association, the Native Sons of the Golden West, the Native Daughters of the Golden West, and "The San Diegans". The flag was officially adopted by the City Council on 16 October 1934. The red-and-gold colour scheme deliberately recalls the flag of Spain, reflecting San Diego's role as the site of the first European settlement on the West Coast (1769).

features.symbolism: red and gold recall the flag of Spain and San Diego's role as the cradle of European settlement in California; gold also evokes the precious metal of the Gold Rush era

features.role: seal of the City of San Diego
features.symbolism: a stylised version of the city seal, entirely contained within the white central stripe; depicts the bell tower of the Mission San Diego de Alcalá (founded in 1769 by Junípero Serra), the Cabrillo Bridge, and other emblems of the city
